description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer tasked with codifying an access-control policy that blocks IP addresses responsible for repeated authentication failures.

    An application log has already been copied to:
        /home/user/simulated_logs/auth-service.log

    1. Scan that file and extract only the records whose text literally contains the substring “login failed” (case-sensitive, exactly that phrase).
    2. From each of those records, capture the IPv4 address that appears after the literal text “ip=” (the IPv4 address is always at the end of the line).
    3. Count how many “login failed” events each distinct IP address generated.

    Policy requirement
    • Threshold for blocking: 3 or more failed login attempts during the observed time window.
    • Any IP below that threshold is placed in “MONITOR” status.

    Output artefacts to produce
    The task is considered complete only after BOTH of the following files are present and match the specified formats exactly.

    A. CSV report  
       Path: /home/user/security/failed_ips.csv  
       File must contain a header line followed by one line per unique IP address that appeared in a “login failed” log line, sorted in ascending lexicographical order of the IP string.  
       Fixed header (must be **exactly** this text including lower-case letters and commas):
       ip,failed_attempts,action  
       For each data line:
            • Column 1: the IPv4 address  
            • Column 2: the integer count of its failed attempts  
            • Column 3: the word BLOCK or MONITOR according to the threshold rule  
       Use plain ASCII, one LF-terminated line each, and **do not** append extra whitespace.

    B. Block-list file  
       Path: /home/user/security/blocklist.conf  
       Contains **only** the IPv4 addresses that received the BLOCK action, one per line, sorted in ascending lexicographical order, terminated with a single trailing newline.  No header, no extra spaces, no blank lines.

    Additional requirements
    • Create the /home/user/security directory if it does not yet exist.  
    • Use only POSIX-compliant command-line tools available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu system (e.g. grep, awk, sed, sort, uniq, cut).  
    • Do not hard-code the expected IP addresses; derive them from the log using a regular-expression pipeline so the solution is generic.  
    • After generating the artefacts, it is fine to display their contents in the terminal for human inspection, but the files themselves will be checked automatically exactly as described above.

    When everything is ready, you may run commands that show the two files to demonstrate success, but remember: the automated grader will rely solely on the correctness and precise formatting of the two files at the paths given above.
truth:
Initial file supplied to the agent
    ---------------------------------
    Path: /home/user/simulated_logs/auth-service.log
    Exact content (with trailing newline at the end of the file):

[2023-09-01 12:00:00] INFO  User login succeeded: user=jdoe   ip=192.168.0.10
[2023-09-01 12:01:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=jdoe   ip=192.168.0.10
[2023-09-01 12:02:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=jdoe   ip=192.168.0.10
[2023-09-01 12:03:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=alice  ip=10.0.0.5
[2023-09-01 12:04:00] INFO  User login succeeded: user=alice  ip=10.0.0.5
[2023-09-01 12:05:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=bob    ip=172.16.1.3
[2023-09-01 12:06:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=bob    ip=172.16.1.3
[2023-09-01 12:07:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=bob    ip=172.16.1.3
[2023-09-01 12:08:00] DEBUG Heartbeat check ok
[2023-09-01 12:09:00] WARN  User login failed:    user=charlie ip=192.168.0.20

    Expected artefacts after successful completion
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/security/failed_ips.csv  
       Content (with a single trailing newline after the last line):
ip,failed_attempts,action
10.0.0.5,1,MONITOR
172.16.1.3,3,BLOCK
192.168.0.10,2,MONITOR
192.168.0.20,1,MONITOR

    2. /home/user/security/blocklist.conf  
       Content (with a single trailing newline after the last line):
172.16.1.3
name: task_000000_affd7a3b
